mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP (GDPC1) signals strong economic expansion, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in periods of GDPC1 growth are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in payrolls (PAYEMS) above 100k signals weak labor market conditions, which depress consumer spending and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in PAYEMS contraction periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) and CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) signal a tightening monetary cycle amid inflationary pressures, which reduces equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in co-moving FEDFUNDS/CPIAUCSL increases are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) during a monetary tightening cycle compresses equity risk premiums, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in FEDFUNDS rising periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates robust manufacturing activity, which supports corporate profits and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in INDPRO growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 500k signals weak demand for residential construction, which reduces economic activity and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in HOUST contraction periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Co-moving declines in consumer spending (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicate weakening demand, which depresses corporate revenues and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS falls > 2% and HOUST falls > 3% in 1 month
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in co-moving RSAFS/HOUST declines are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in unemployment (UNRATE) above 8% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, which reduces consumer confidence and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in UNRATE spikes are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
